apps.name: Steam
apps.bio: Valve's PC gaming storefront and library manager.
apps.description: Steam is the largest digital distribution platform for PC games, with a library of tens of thousands of titles, community features, cloud saves, and the Steam Workshop for mods. Installing it first gets your game library, friends list, and controller configs back in one shot.

apps.name: Epic Games Launcher
apps.bio: Epic Games Store client for buying and launching games.
apps.description: The Epic Games Launcher is the home of Fortnite, the Unreal Engine, and Epic's storefront — famous for giving away free games every week. If you've been claiming those freebies, this launcher is how you get your whole collection back.
apps.notes: Epic dropped official macOS launcher support in 2019; Windows-only entry.

apps.name: GOG Galaxy
apps.bio: DRM-free game library and launcher from GOG.com.
apps.description: GOG Galaxy is CD Projekt's launcher for DRM-free games, and its 2.0 version can unify libraries from every other launcher (Steam, Epic, Xbox, PlayStation) into a single view. Great as both a store client and an all-in-one game hub.
apps.notes: Verified live 2026-07-08 (200, application/octet-stream).

apps.name: Wargaming Game Center
apps.bio: Launcher for World of Tanks, World of Warships, and other Wargaming titles.
apps.description: Wargaming Game Center manages World of Tanks, World of Warships, World of Warplanes, and the rest of Wargaming's catalog — handling installs, updates, region switching, and account sign-in from one launcher.
apps.notes: wgc.wargaming.net doesn't resolve at all (dead DNS) — the previous URL was broken, not just the wrong app. Switched to redirect.wargaming.net's stable NA-region install redirect, verified live end-to-end (200, application/x-msdownload, ~4.8MB).

apps.name: Battle.net
apps.bio: Blizzard's launcher for Overwatch, Diablo, WoW, and more.
apps.description: Battle.net is Blizzard's launcher for Overwatch, Diablo, World of Warcraft, StarCraft, and Hearthstone, plus Activision titles like Call of Duty. Handles game installs, patching, and Blizzard's friends/chat layer.
apps.notes: Verified live 2026-07-08 — getInstallerForGame redirects to the current versioned installer on downloader.battle.net for both OSes.

apps.name: Ubisoft Connect
apps.bio: Ubisoft's launcher, storefront, and cross-game rewards platform.
apps.description: Ubisoft Connect is Ubisoft's launcher and ecosystem for Assassin's Creed, Rainbow Six Siege, Far Cry, and the rest of their catalog — with cross-game rewards, cloud saves, and cross-progression on supported titles.
apps.notes: The old ubistatic-a.akamaihd.net URL now returns 401 — switched to Ubisoft's static3.cdn.ubi.com launcher installer, verified live 2026-07-08 (200, application/octet-stream).

apps.name: EA App
apps.bio: EA's launcher for FIFA, Battlefield, Apex Legends, and other EA titles.
apps.description: The EA app is Electronic Arts' launcher (Origin's successor) for FIFA/EA Sports FC, Battlefield, Apex Legends, The Sims, and EA Play subscriptions. Required for most EA titles on PC even when bought through Steam.
apps.notes: Verified live 2026-07-08 (200, binary/octet-stream).

apps.name: Battlestate Games Launcher
apps.bio: Battlestate's launcher for Escape from Tarkov.
apps.description: The Battlestate Games Launcher installs, updates, and launches Escape from Tarkov (and Arena). Required to play — game downloads, patching, and account sign-in all go through it.
apps.notes: launcher.escapefromtarkov.com/launcher/download is the versionless endpoint their own site uses; it 302s to the current installer on cdn-*.eft-store.com. Verified live 2026-07-09 (200, application/octet-stream). prod.escapefromtarkov.com/launcher/download looks similar but returns a 403 JSON body.

apps.name: Riot Client
apps.bio: Shared launcher for League of Legends, Valorant, and other Riot games.
apps.description: The Riot Client is the shared launcher for League of Legends, Valorant, Teamfight Tactics, and Legends of Runeterra. One install covers sign-in, updates, and launching for every Riot game on your machine.
apps.notes: Verified live against the real riotcdn.net installer URL (installs the shared Riot Client used by all Riot games).

apps.name: TestMem5
apps.bio: RAM stability tester used for tuning memory timings.
apps.description: TestMem5 (TM5) is the memory-overclocking community's stability tester of choice — it hammers RAM with configurable test patterns (like the popular anta777 configs) and surfaces errors that milder tests miss. Essential for validating DRAM timing tweaks before calling them stable.
apps.notes: testmem5.com/download 404s (was always a placeholder). Switched to the actively maintained CoolCmd/TestMem5 GitHub project (open-source TM5 continuation with releases) — verified live 2026-07-08 via the GitHub API, latest release ships a TestMem5.7z asset.

apps.name: ZenTimings
apps.bio: Reads and tweaks live DRAM timings on AMD Ryzen platforms.
apps.description: ZenTimings shows every primary, secondary, and tertiary DRAM timing plus voltages on AMD Ryzen systems, read straight from the memory controller. The standard companion tool for Ryzen RAM tuning — screenshot it and any overclocking forum knows exactly what you're running.

apps.name: Cinebench R23
apps.bio: Maxon's CPU benchmark built on the Cinema 4D render engine.
apps.description: Cinebench R23 is Maxon's CPU benchmark built on the Cinema 4D render engine, producing single-core and multi-core scores that are the lingua franca of CPU comparisons. Its 10-minute throttling test doubles as a quick thermal sanity check.
apps.notes: Upgraded from R20: Maxon no longer hosts the R20 zip anywhere official, but R23 has a stable official URL at installer.maxon.net — verified live 2026-07-08 (200, application/x-zip-compressed).

apps.name: CPU-Z
apps.bio: Reports detailed CPU, motherboard, and RAM specs.
apps.description: CPU-Z is the classic system-information utility: exact CPU model and stepping, live clock speeds and voltages, motherboard and BIOS info, and per-stick RAM details including XMP profiles. Includes a simple built-in bench for quick comparisons.
apps.notes: Resolves the latest version dynamically: scrapes the first (newest) '-en.exe' link off cpuid.com's CPU-Z page, then rewrites it against download.cpuid.com, which serves the real binary (the www. path only serves an HTML interstitial). Verified live 2026-07-08.

apps.name: HWiNFO
apps.bio: In-depth hardware monitoring for every sensor on the system.
apps.description: HWiNFO is the most thorough hardware monitor on Windows — every sensor your system exposes (temperatures, clocks, voltages, fan speeds, power draw) with logging, graphing, and tray readouts. The go-to for diagnosing thermals and validating an overclock under load.
apps.notes: Resolves the latest version dynamically: hwinfo.com/download has the current 64-bit installer link (files/hwi64_*.exe) in static HTML. Verified live 2026-07-08 (200, application/x-msdownload).

apps.name: HWMonitor x64
apps.bio: Lightweight hardware monitoring for temps, voltages, and fan speeds.
apps.description: HWMonitor is CPUID's lightweight sensor readout — temperatures, voltages, fan speeds, and power in a simple tree view with min/max columns. Less exhaustive than HWiNFO but faster to open and read at a glance.
apps.notes: Resolves the latest version dynamically: scrapes the first (newest) installer link off cpuid.com's HWMonitor page, then rewrites it against download.cpuid.com, which serves the real binary. Verified live 2026-07-08.

apps.name: NVIDIA Profile Inspector
apps.bio: Unlocks hidden NVIDIA driver settings not exposed in the GeForce control panel.
apps.description: NVIDIA Profile Inspector exposes every hidden setting in the NVIDIA driver's per-game profiles — frame-rate limits, anti-aliasing flags, latency tweaks, and options the official control panel never shows. A staple for squeezing more out of GeForce drivers.

apps.name: OCCT
apps.bio: Stress-tests CPU, GPU, and PSU stability under load.
apps.description: OCCT stress-tests CPU, GPU, memory, and even power-supply stability with error detection built in, and shows live sensor graphs while it runs. Its combined CPU+GPU power test is one of the fastest ways to expose an unstable overclock or a failing PSU.
apps.notes: Verified live 2026-07-08 — redirects to /download-bin/OCCT.zip and serves application/octet-stream.

apps.name: Prime95
apps.bio: Classic CPU stress-testing and stability tool.
apps.description: Prime95 is the legendary CPU torture test from the Great Internet Mersenne Prime Search. Its Small FFTs mode produces near-worst-case heat and load, making it the traditional final exam for CPU overclocks — if it survives Prime95 overnight, it's stable.
apps.notes: The old unversioned prime95.zip URL 404s now. Resolves dynamically by scraping the current win64 build link off mersenne.org's download page ([href$='.win64.zip'] deliberately excludes the .service.zip variant). Verified live 2026-07-08.

apps.name: Timer Resolution
apps.bio: Forces a lower Windows timer resolution for smoother frame pacing.
apps.description: SetTimerResolution forces Windows to run at a finer timer resolution (e.g. 0.5ms), which can reduce micro-stutter and input latency in some games. A small, single-purpose tool popular in competitive-FPS tuning guides.
apps.notes: Tool is published as "SetTimerResolution" under this repo, not a "TimerResolution*.zip" as previously assumed.

apps.name: ASRock Timing Configurator
apps.bio: ASRock's GUI for tuning memory timings alongside ZenTimings.
apps.description: ASRock Timing Configurator reads and adjusts DRAM timings from inside Windows on ASRock boards (and often works on other AMD boards too), letting you experiment with memory timings without a BIOS round-trip for every change.
apps.notes: This is ASRock's tool (old timingconfigurator.com domain is dead) — hosted on ASRock's own download server, pinned at v4.0.4 which is its long-final release. Verified live 2026-07-08 (200, application/zip) plus a network-hitting live test.

apps.name: Display Driver Uninstaller (DDU)
apps.bio: Cleanly removes GPU drivers before installing a fresh version.
apps.description: Display Driver Uninstaller (DDU) scrubs GPU drivers — NVIDIA, AMD, or Intel — completely from the system, including registry leftovers that normal uninstalls miss. Run it in safe mode before a fresh driver install to cure crashes, artifacts, and mystery performance loss.
apps.notes: wagnardsoft.com and guru3d.com are both Cloudflare-JS-challenged (unfetchable without a real browser), so DDU resolves via majorgeeks.com's mirror page. Its per-session tokenized file URL only exists inside an HTML comment there — hence the html_regex resolver, which regexes the raw body instead of using a CSS selector. Verified live 2026-07-08 end-to-end (fresh resolve → cookie-less download, 200 application/x-msdos-program) plus a network-hitting live test.

apps.name: NanaZip
apps.bio: Modern 7-Zip fork with a Windows 11-style UI and better compression.
apps.description: NanaZip is a modern fork of 7-Zip built for Windows 11: same excellent compression engine and format support, but with context-menu integration in the new right-click menu, dark mode, and an actively maintained codebase.
apps.notes: Corrected from "NanoZip" (an unrelated, likely-abandoned tool) to NanaZip, the actively maintained modern 7-Zip fork. Verified against the live M2Team/NanaZip GitHub repo.

apps.name: WinRAR
apps.bio: The classic RAR/ZIP archive manager.
apps.description: WinRAR is the long-standing archive manager behind the RAR format, with solid handling of ZIP and most other formats, recovery records for damaged archives, and the world's most famously ignorable trial dialog.
apps.notes: Verified against the live win-rar.com/download.html page structure.

apps.name: 7-Zip
apps.bio: Free, open-source archive manager with excellent 7z compression.
apps.description: 7-Zip is the free, open-source archiver whose 7z format routinely out-compresses ZIP and RAR. Tiny, fast, scriptable from the command line, and handles virtually every archive format you'll ever encounter.
apps.notes: 7-Zip moved its releases to GitHub (ip7z/7zip) in 2024; verified against the live repo.

apps.name: Bitwarden
apps.bio: Open-source password manager with browser and app sync.
apps.description: Bitwarden is an open-source password manager with end-to-end encryption, cross-device sync, browser extensions, and passkey support — free tier included. Getting it installed early means every other login during your post-wipe setup is one click away.

apps.name: Windscribe
apps.bio: VPN and ad/tracker blocker with a generous free tier.
apps.description: Windscribe is a VPN with a genuinely useful free tier (10GB/month), a built-in ad and tracker blocker called R.O.B.E.R.T., and clients for every platform. Good for geo-shifting, public-Wi-Fi safety, and keeping your ISP out of your browsing.
apps.notes: windscribe.com/download itself is JS-rendered (Next.js, no .exe link in the raw HTML), but windscribe.com/install/desktop/<os> is a stable, versionless redirect straight to the current installer — verified live, no JS execution needed.

apps.name: Visual Studio Code
apps.bio: Microsoft's free, extensible source-code editor.
apps.description: Visual Studio Code is Microsoft's free, open-source code editor — the most used editor in the world, with an extension for every language and workflow, built-in Git, debugging, and remote development. The first install of any dev setup.
apps.notes: macOS: the old os=darwin slug 404s (Microsoft split it into universal/arm64/x64 builds) — switched to the canonical update.code.visualstudio.com latest endpoint for the universal build. Verified live 2026-07-08.

apps.name: PyCharm
apps.bio: JetBrains' full-featured Python IDE.
apps.description: PyCharm is JetBrains' Python IDE with first-class refactoring, debugging, test running, and virtualenv management. This installs the free Community edition — everything you need for serious Python work without a license.
apps.notes: jetbrains.com/pycharm/download itself is JS-rendered, but JetBrains publishes a stable, versionless download API (the same one their own site's download button calls) that redirects straight to the current PyCharm Community installer — verified live, no scraping needed.

apps.name: NetLimiter
apps.bio: Per-application bandwidth monitoring and throttling for Windows.
apps.description: NetLimiter monitors network traffic per application and lets you throttle, prioritize, or block any process's connection. Perfect for keeping a game's ping stable while something else downloads, or catching an app quietly phoning home.
apps.notes: The old unversioned nl5setup.exe URL 404s now (their file host serves versioned filenames like netlimiter-5.3.26.0.exe). Resolves dynamically by scraping the current installer link off netlimiter.com's download page. Verified live 2026-07-08.

apps.name: Notepad++
apps.bio: Lightweight source-code editor with syntax highlighting for dozens of languages.
apps.description: Notepad++ is the featherweight text editor every Windows power user keeps around: instant startup, syntax highlighting for dozens of languages, powerful find/replace across files, and endless plugins. Ideal for quick edits where an IDE is overkill.

apps.name: PuTTY
apps.bio: SSH and telnet client for connecting to remote machines.
apps.description: PuTTY is the venerable SSH and telnet client for Windows — a single small binary that gets you onto remote servers, serial consoles, and network gear. Decades old, still the default answer for a quick SSH session.
apps.notes: The old URL 404'd: the 'latest/' directory alias updates but the filename inside it is versioned (was pinned to 0.78, current is 0.84+). Now resolves dynamically by scraping the installer link off PuTTY's own latest.html page (static HTML). Verified live 2026-07-08.

apps.name: PowerToys
apps.bio: Microsoft's set of power-user utilities — FancyZones, PowerToys Run, and more.
apps.description: Microsoft PowerToys is a free bundle of power-user utilities: FancyZones window layouts, PowerToys Run launcher, Keyboard Manager remapping, PowerRename batch renaming, Always on Top, and more. One install fixes a dozen small Windows annoyances.

apps.name: Process Explorer
apps.bio: Advanced Task Manager replacement showing full process trees and handles.
apps.description: Process Explorer is Sysinternals' supercharged Task Manager: full process trees, which handles and DLLs a process has open, per-process GPU/CPU graphs, and VirusTotal integration. The first tool to reach for when something mysterious is running.

apps.name: Autoruns
apps.bio: Shows and manages everything configured to start automatically on Windows.
apps.description: Autoruns (Sysinternals) shows everything configured to start automatically on Windows — services, drivers, scheduled tasks, shell extensions, browser helpers, all of it — and lets you disable each with a checkbox. The definitive tool for cleaning up boot cruft.

apps.name: Brave Browser
apps.bio: Chromium-based browser with built-in ad and tracker blocking.
apps.description: Brave is a Chromium-based browser with aggressive ad and tracker blocking built in, so sites load faster without extensions. Full Chrome extension compatibility, optional crypto features you can ignore, and solid default privacy.
apps.notes: referrals.brave.com stopped responding (connection errors in the wild), so both OSes now pull from Brave's official GitHub releases instead — verified live 2026-07-08 via the GitHub API (BraveBrowserSetup.exe / Brave-Browser-universal.dmg on the latest release).

apps.name: LibreWolf
apps.bio: Privacy-hardened Firefox fork with tracking stripped out by default.
apps.description: LibreWolf is Firefox with the privacy dial turned to max out of the box: telemetry stripped, tracking protection hardened, and uBlock Origin preinstalled. For people who want Firefox without trusting themselves to configure it.
apps.notes: Verified against the live librewolf.net installation pages; targets the x86_64 build on both platforms.

apps.name: Zen Browser
apps.bio: Firefox-based browser with a vertical-tab, workspace-driven UI.
apps.description: Zen Browser is a Firefox-based browser rethought around vertical tabs, workspaces, and split views, with an Arc-like minimalist design. Open-source and community-driven — for people who want their browser to feel like a workspace, not a tab strip.
apps.notes: Verified against the live zen-browser/desktop GitHub releases.

apps.name: Discord
apps.bio: Voice, video, and text chat built for communities and gaming.
apps.description: Discord is the de facto voice, video, and text platform for gaming communities and group chats — servers, screen sharing, and low-latency voice channels. First thing to reinstall if your friends live in it.
apps.notes: Fixed from the previous canary-build endpoint to Discord's stable release endpoint.

apps.name: Telegram
apps.bio: Cloud-based messaging with large group chats and channels.
apps.description: Telegram is a fast, cloud-based messenger with unlimited synced history across devices, huge group chats and channels, and a desktop client that doesn't need your phone online. Great for file sharing with its generous 2GB-per-file limit.

apps.name: TeamSpeak
apps.bio: Low-latency voice chat popular with gaming communities.
apps.description: TeamSpeak is the classic self-hosted voice chat for gaming clans — extremely low latency, military-grade codec quality, and full control when you run your own server. Still the choice for communities that outgrew Discord's voice quality.
apps.notes: teamspeak.com/en/downloads is NOT actually JS-rendered — the real download URL is right there in the static HTML, just on a <button data-url="..."> instead of an <a href="...">, which is why the original a.download-windows selector never matched anything. Needs a browser-like User-Agent header or the site 403s. Verified live.

apps.name: Restart Audio Service
apps.bio: Restarts the Windows Audio service — fixes most "no sound" glitches without a reboot.
apps.description: Generates a PowerShell script that restarts the Windows Audio service — the classic fix when sound suddenly dies, a device switch goes wrong, or an app hijacks the output. Self-elevates via UAC and saves you a full reboot.

apps.name: Kill Valorant Process
apps.bio: Force-kills lingering Valorant/Vanguard processes that block a clean relaunch.
apps.description: Generates a PowerShell script that force-kills a stuck VALORANT process, which the Vanguard anti-cheat can leave lingering after a crash and block relaunching. Self-elevates via UAC so the kill actually sticks.

apps.name: Cursor Packs
apps.bio: Custom cursor schemes — installs via install.inf and shows Active in green once applied.

apps.name: Fonts
apps.bio: Curated font packs with live previews before installing.

apps.name: Audio & EQ Profiles
apps.bio: Peace parametric EQ plus a Sennheiser HD650 "Oratory" profile.

apps.name: Steam Profiles
apps.bio: Custom Steam profile showcases and workshop packs, with previews.

apps.name: Windows Sounds
apps.bio: Custom Windows system sound schemes.

apps.name: Wallpapers & Profile Pics
apps.bio: Desktop wallpapers, profile pictures, and banners, with previews.

apps.name: Windows Themes (deprecated)
apps.bio: Custom .msstyles themes — deprecated: these only worked on Windows 10.
apps.description: The Paranoid Android theme pack (.theme/.msstyles + patched shellstyle.dll) targeted Windows 10's theme engine and doesn't work on Windows 11. Kept listed for the archive, but not installable.
